archetypes.verdict: The simulation shows strong scientific and patient enthusiasm around CRISPR-Cure-7, with the strongest signal coming from the claimed 85% remission rate and multi-pathway targeting approach.
archetypes.would_move_price: Full Phase 1 data, durability of remission, and transparent adverse-event reporting would move confidence up; severe off-target edits or weak Phase 2 replication would move it down.

archetypes.verdict: The regulatory voice in the simulation prevents runaway hype: Phase 1 remission data is promising, but gene-editing safety, follow-up duration, and manufacturing controls dominate the approval path.
archetypes.would_move_price: A clean FDA meeting package with predefined safety monitoring and scalable CMC evidence would raise confidence; unresolved off-target risk would sharply lower it.

archetypes.verdict: Market demand is obvious, but payer adoption depends on cost, durability, and whether remission translates into fewer downstream oncology expenses.
archetypes.would_move_price: Evidence of durable remission plus a credible outcomes-based pricing model would move price up; unaffordable manufacturing or narrow eligibility would move it down.

archetypes.verdict: The scenario is compelling but fragile: a 50-patient Phase 1 signal cannot establish standard-of-care status across metastatic cancers without randomized, longer-horizon evidence.
archetypes.would_move_price: Independent Phase 2 replication across cancer types would raise confidence; regression toward typical oncology response rates would lower it.

headline_catalysts: Phase 2 replication of the 85% remission signal across larger metastatic cancer cohorts.
Transparent off-target editing and long-term safety data.
Manufacturing and reimbursement plan strong enough for broad oncology access.

derived_topics.title: Durable remission validation for CRISPR-Cure-7
derived_topics.rationale: Tests whether the Phase 1 remission signal survives larger, longer studies.

derived_topics.title: Off-target edit surveillance in multi-pathway oncology CRISPR
derived_topics.rationale: Safety is the main blocker to standard-of-care adoption.

derived_topics.title: Payer adoption model for curative metastatic cancer therapies
derived_topics.rationale: Even effective therapies need reimbursement logic to reach patients.

topics.title: Durable remission validation for CRISPR-Cure-7
topics.rationale: Tests whether the Phase 1 remission signal survives larger, longer studies.

topics.rationale: Safety is the main blocker to standard-of-care adoption.

peer_reviews.summary: Promising simulated market reaction, but standard-of-care claims require randomized data beyond a small Phase 1 cohort.
criticisms: Phase 1 sample size is too small for broad metastatic cancer claims.
No durability or adverse-event distribution is included.
required_fixes: Add cohort size, follow-up duration, cancer-type stratification, and off-target monitoring details.
suggested_experiments: Run a simulated Phase 2 design with response decay, adverse events, and stratified tumor types.

peer_reviews.summary: The patient enthusiasm signal is credible, but access, cost, and safety communication need equal weight.
criticisms: The simulation underweights affordability and eligibility constraints.
required_fixes: Model reimbursement delays and compassionate-use pathways.
suggested_experiments: Add payer denial and accelerated-access branches to the public reaction simulation.

simulations.title: Market Belief Shift
simulations.description: Animates the prediction market moving from 65% to 78% as agents react to CRISPR-Cure-7 news.
simulations.rationale: Makes the core simulation result visible: enthusiasm rises, but scrutiny keeps the curve from going vertical.

simulations.title: Regulatory Pressure Field
simulations.description: Shows optimism and safety scrutiny pushing against each other during expert review.
simulations.rationale: The simulated blocker is not lack of interest; it is whether safety and evidence mature fast enough.

simulations.title: Patient Access Gradient
simulations.description: Maps patient demand through reimbursement, manufacturing, and eligibility constraints.
simulations.rationale: A curative claim only matters if patients can actually reach the therapy.
